title: Cursor Mundi
summary: The repository XML copies U-M CME item AJT8128.0001.001, a 2006 raw uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding of a four-volume U-M carrier that rearranges seven distinct Richard Morris EETS issued parts (OS 57, 59, 62, 66, 68, 99, and 101; 1874-1893). The historical edition presents the anonymous Middle English Cursor Mundi in parallel witness streams, additions, and appendices, including an incomplete Fairfax Cato's Morals. Forty scoped relations preserve source switches such as Trinity/RCPE and distinguish the Morris composite, original issued parts, U-M rebound carriers, Toronto scan tranches, digital encoding, package, facsimiles, and physical witnesses. Forty-five exact ignored local source objects are mapped with full-object coverage and target-work portions where present. No relation asserts direct consultation of a present codex without evidence.

evidence.citation: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, 17 U.S.C. § 104A, Copyright in restored works
evidence.summary: For a restored work, copyright subsists only for the remainder of the U.S. term the work otherwise would have received had it never entered the U.S. public domain.
notes: Official OLRC current-code evidence; it does not by itself establish that any particular Morris part is a restored work or identify a source country.

evidence.citation: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, 17 U.S.C. § 304, Duration of copyright: Subsisting copyrights
evidence.summary: Section 304 preserves and extends qualifying pre-1978 terms, with a maximum 95-year duration from the date copyright was originally secured for copyrights within the provision’s renewal framework.
notes: Used as a conservative upper-bound term calculation, not as a claim that the London publications were first published or secured in the United States.
